name: Bistro Beef Steak
recipeIngredient: 1 pound beef top sirloin, boneless, cut into 3/4 inch thick slices
1 large red onion, sliced into 1/4-inch thick rings
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim fat from beef steak. Cut steak lengthwise in half and then crosswise into 1/2-inch thick strips.
2. In a medium bowl, combine beef, parsley, garlic, and pepper; toss to coat. Set aside.
3. Cut onion into 1/4-inch thick slices. Separate into rings.
4. Heat oil over medium-high heat in a large non-stick skillet until hot. Add onion and cook for 3 to 5 minutes or until crisp-tender. Remove to serving platter; keep warm.
5. In the same skillet, add beef and stir-fry for 2 minutes or until outside surface is no longer pink. Season with salt, if desired. Place beef on top of onion.
6. Add wine to skillet and cook until browned bits attached to the surface are dissolved and liquid thickens slightly. Pour sauce over beef.
7. Serve with steamed new potatoes.
Cook's notes:
In France, bistro cooking is casual, home-style food bursting with hearty flavors. Steak and steamed new potatoes with a red wine sauce brings bistro fare to your table.
To steam new potatoes, place steamer basket in 1/2 inch of water (water should not touch the bottom of the basket). Place 10 to 12 new potatoes (about 1 1/2 pounds) in the basket. Cover tightly and heat until boiling; reduce heat. Steam for 18 to 22 minutes or until tender.
Nutrition information per serving:
Calories: 406, Fat: 10 grams, Protein: 31 grams, Carbohydrates: 46 grams, Cholesterol: 76 mg, Sodium: 73 mg
